name: Refactor Execute and Create Default

tasks.title: Extract behavioral guidance from execute.md into default.md
tasks.description: Extract behavioral guidance from execute.md into default.md
tasks.body: Create internal/project/templates/prompts/classes/default.md containing all language-agnostic SWE behavioral guidance currently embedded in execute.md. This includes: Phase C coding conventions (file count limits, decomposition signals, package rename prohibition, deletion verification, deliverable checking), Phase D validation rules (build, test, format, lint/vet, spec cross-reference, stdlib check), and any other behavioral guidance that shapes HOW the model approaches implementation. After extraction, execute.md must contain ONLY structural concerns: phase ordering (A through K), boundary rules (.wolfcastle/ access, working directory), terminal marker protocol, commit protocol, AAR writing instructions, ADR/spec documentation instructions, and sub-task decomposition mechanics. Zero language-specific or SWE-best-practice guidance should remain in execute.md. The default.md prompt must follow the same authoring rules as all class prompts: no wolfcastle-specific content (no terminal markers, breadcrumbs, audit gaps, deliverables, specs, ADRs, AARs, yielding, or system mechanics). It describes how to write good code, not how to interact with wolfcastle.
acceptance_criteria: execute.md contains zero language-specific or SWE-best-practice guidance
default.md contains all extracted behavioral guidance in class-prompt format
default.md contains zero wolfcastle-specific content (no terminal markers

tasks.title: Wire default.md as fallback class guidance in ContextBuilder
tasks.description: Wire default.md as fallback class guidance in ContextBuilder
tasks.body: Modify ContextBuilder.Build() in internal/pipeline/context_builder.go so that every task receives class guidance. Current behavior: class guidance is injected only when task.Class is non-empty and Resolve succeeds; otherwise no class section appears. New behavior: (1) When task.Class is non-empty, resolve it via ClassRepository. If resolution succeeds, inject the resolved prompt. If resolution fails, fall back to default.md. (2) When task.Class is empty or unset, inject default.md directly. The fallback resolution should call classes.prompts.ResolveRaw('prompts/classes', 'default.md') or equivalent. The net effect: no task ever runs without a ## Class Guidance section. Update existing tests in context_builder_test.go to reflect the new fallback behavior. Add test cases for: empty class resolves to default.md, unknown class falls back to default.md, known class with missing prompt falls back to default.md.
acceptance_criteria: Tasks with empty Class field receive default.md content in Class Guidance section
Tasks with unresolvable Class field receive default.md content as fallback
Tasks with valid Class field receive their class-specific prompt

tasks.title: Audit
tasks.description: Verify all work in Refactor Execute and Create Default is complete and correct

audit_enrichment: Verify execute.md contains no language-specific guidance (no mentions of gofmt, prettier, black, cargo, pytest, etc.) and no SWE best-practice advice (file count limits, stdlib checks, etc.)
Verify default.md contains zero wolfcastle system references (grep for wolfcastle, WOLFCASTLE_, breadcrumb, audit, deliverable, AAR, ADR, spec, yield)
Verify ContextBuilder injects default.md for tasks with empty Class field and for tasks with unresolvable Class field

breadcrumbs.text: Created classes/default.md with extracted SWE behavioral guidance; refactored execute.md to contain only structural concerns

task-0001.objective: Extract SWE behavioral guidance from execute.md into a standalone default.md class prompt
task-0001.what_happened: Created internal/project/templates/prompts/classes/default.md with all language-agnostic SWE guidance (scope/focus, structural integrity, validation steps). Stripped Phase C of coding conventions (package rename prohibition, deletion verification, decomposition signals) and replaced Phase D's full validation checklist with a one-line reference to class guidance. Removed the package-rename rule from the Rules section since it's now in default.md.
went_well: Clean separation: execute.md is now purely structural/mechanical, default.md is purely about how to write good code
The go:embed all:templates directive picks up the new classes/ directory automatically with no code changes
improvements: The 8-file limit appears in both execute.md (as a decomposition trigger) and default.md (as scope guidance). This is intentional since they serve different purposes (wolfcastle decomposition mechanic vs SWE focus principle) but could be confusing.
action_items: Verify that ContextBuilder injects default.md when a task has no class set, or decide whether default.md is only used as fallback for class resolution
